C[Himiko: Hi, would you like me to give you a review after?
ontheqt: Normally I would, however tonight (here) I am going to call it a night after the game
Himiko: would you like the odd comment during te game if I see something I can help you with?
C[Himiko: would you like the odd comment during te game if I see something I can help you with?
C[Himiko: okay, best thing to do in the opening is to play open corners first
Himiko: sometimes pros do strange things, but for now, just take the other open corner :-)
C[ontheqt: i keep forgetting to submit move
Himiko: ah, just hit the ""check"" mark on the left side
C[ontheqt: stone sounds need some work
Himiko: you see this shape, white has ""hit you on the head of two stones""
Himiko: that is bad for you
C[Himiko: better to have played C2 instead of D4
C[Himiko: F7 was quite a nice move - shape good and builds influence
C[Himiko: E17 was too slow - best to play D17 there
C[Himiko: position is more important than material
ontheqt: I felt that a double atari might erode my little base
Himiko: I could have captured a stone or two, but L10 is a very big point
C[Himiko: you can see how it is the border of my centre and it provides a springboard to do nasty things to the lower side of the board
ontheqt: well, would I be better served invading the QR 12-14  area or K4?  I always struggle with fighting for enemy territory vs low lying fruit
Himiko: arond Q12 you will probably get killed
Himiko: it should be too late to invade..although I often miss my chances
C[Himiko: nice forcing moves by you
Himiko: no real choice for me but to defend myself
C[ontheqt: regrettably I did not make earth shattering moves with it
ontheqt: was thinking I should have played C13 instead
Himiko: don`t think in all or nothing terms
Himiko: sometimes just getting the opponent to reply is a good result
C[Himiko: you don`t have to be killing or destroying to be succeeding in go
C[ontheqt: I really hate making empty triangles.  It's something that comes from outside of  what books say.
C[Himiko: don`t respond - better to have carried your own threat at B12
Himiko: in the endgame, if it`s sente for both sides, ""double sente"", it`s bigger than just defending ""gote""
ontheqt: overthinking you.  Meaning, I decieded you left B12 open because it wasn't the priority  I thought it was
Himiko: the thing about ""sente"" though, is that you don`t *have* to respond to sente
Himiko: which is kind of hard to get your head around but...
ontheqt: it has to be a convincing argument I suppose
Himiko: sente is creating threats and taking the initiative, but the opponent can always ignore those threats
Himiko: the only time when you really have to answer is if the threat is so big it will lose you the game instantly
Himiko: even if I had followed up S7, I cannot kill your lower right
ontheqt: so, the person who responds as his opponent intended is therefor in gote?
Himiko: so all you had to lose there was a bit of territory
Himiko: which you could have compensated by taking some of mine
Himiko: gote means a move that does not need an answer
ontheqt: I am frightfully greedy of what I percieve is mine.
Himiko: there are four qualities of a good move, in my opinion
ontheqt: so, not gote strictly speaking but could be gote
Himiko: 1) Active 2) Has a follow-up aim 3) Is reasonable 4) As large as possible
Himiko: yes, sente and gote are to some extend subjective
C[ontheqt: somewhat generic criteria.  On the board, what makes a move ""active""
C[Himiko: for instance, expaning territory or creating threats
C[Himiko: no choice for me here but to reply in gote...allowing youto keep going into the corner would be dangerous
C[Himiko: I made some mistakes...L3 was rash because I had to fix up the cut at N5 by playing P4 and N3
Himiko: better to prepare first, rather than to make a threat and have to go back and repair
Himiko: so here`s a motto ""Prepare instead of repair!""
C[ontheqt: K18 just took the wind out of my sails
Himiko: in the end game, look for moves that expand both sides` territories
Himiko: they tend to be very big
Himiko: because they add points to one side and take points away from the other
C[Himiko: okay, here is a ""prepare, not repair"" move
ontheqt: dont really know how to respond to K18
C[Himiko: now I can play sente at K2 or O2
C[Himiko: if I had playd the other way around, then I have had to go back and repair
C[Himiko: this is a border point
ontheqt: yup.  Was just looking at that and thinking of your diving board comment
C[Himiko: possibly  my move is too solid
Himiko: even though M12 is dead, it could have caused shortage of liberties problems for me had I stretched too much in the centre
ontheqt: it was firm.  I see the benefit of it for you in defense.  Doesn't fit your style of offense (from what I've seen in this game)
C[Himiko: maybe I could have play J7 but I tend to be wary of cutting aji around K9
Himiko: I wanted to play in that area first
C[Himiko: I have no choice but to answer if I want to live
Himiko: your move is very big
ontheqt: yes, J7 or in that offensive area is what I was waiting for....with some dread
C[ontheqt: I still am, I suppose
C[Himiko: see that my style is quite simple - I am doing nothing too drastice
Himiko: the idea is that preparing attacks is stronger than launching in straightaway
ontheqt: oh?  I'm not to sure about that statement.  the upper right corner is pretty drastic.
Himiko: but I built it quite slowly
Himiko: I have not killed anything of yours or even attacked you severely
ontheqt: I think M13 was pretty drastic.
ontheqt: however I am very thankful.  A very fun game.
Himiko: I hope I have helped you

ontheqt: my problem is this.  I don't see any value in the F11 area.
